crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: 40% surge in E4W volumes signals structural gearshift beyond West Asia
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Electric four-wheeler (E4W) adoption is gathering pace, with average monthly volumes rising ~40% to around 26,000 units during the three months ending May 2026 – an all-time high compared with the fiscal 2026 average. While recent fuel price increases, triggered by the West Asia conflict, have impro

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Private defence players' growth to be steady at 15-16% on Atmanirbhar drive
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Revenue of private sector companies in the defence sector will grow 15-16% this fiscal, similar to last fiscal, supported by a healthy order book of ~Rs 50,000 crore. The growth rides on the government's Atmanirbhar initiative to procure 75% of India's defence requirements from domestic sources.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Auto component margins seen at 10.5-11% amid West Asia conflict
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Operating margins of Indian auto component sector is expected to moderate by 100-150 basis points this fiscal from around 12% last year, with the West Asia conflict driving up input prices and freight costs across domestic and overseas markets.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Price hikes to haul cable and wire revenue up 28-30% this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: After a strong volume led growth (20%+) last fiscal, manufacturers of cables and wires (C&W) will grow even faster this fiscal albeit on sharper price hikes to pass on a steep rise in prices of key raw materials. While volume growth will moderate, it will remain healthy supported by demand from infr

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: West Asia conflict to propel primary aluminium profitability to decadal high
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: A global aluminium supply deficit, triggered by the West Asia conflict, is poised to drive aluminium prices to record highs, improving realizations for Indian primary aluminium manufacturers. This, along with contained cost of production, is likely to uplift profitability of players to a decadal hig

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Current a/c surplus halves on-year
crisil-research.description: India’s current account rebounded to a surplus of $7.1 billion (0.7% of the gross domestic product, or GDP) in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026, compared with a $15.5 billion deficit in the third quarter.

crisil-research.title: Swing stabiliser
crisil-research.description: The recently approved Rs 10,000 crore Price Stabilisation Fund (PSF) for oil marketing companies (OMCs) will help absorb some of the increase in fuel procurement costs when crude prices spike. This will ensure stable aviation turbine 6fuel (ATF) prices for Indian airline carriers by dampening the im

crisil-research.title: Indian Economy: Price tentacles amid growth moderation
crisil-research.description: Crisil expects India’s gross domestic product (GDP) to grow 6.6% this fiscal, with the average Consumer Price Index (CPI)-based inflation at 5.1% as the West Asia conflict extends to the third month with no lasting solution in sight.

crisil-research.title: Global Economy: The impact percolates
crisil-research.description: Real gross domestic product growth picked up in the first quarter of 2026 in the United States (US; annualized 2% vs 0.5% in the previous quarter), the United Kingdom (UK; seasonally adjusted 0.6% vs 0.2%) and China (5% vs 4.5%).

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Resilient, so far
crisil-research.description: The National Statistical Office (NSO) has estimated gross domestic product (GDP) growth for fiscal 2026 at 7.7%, a tad higher than the second advance estimate of 7.6% released in February

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Resounding pause
crisil-research.description: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) kept policy rates unchanged at its June meeting. The repo rate stands at 5.25%, standing deposit facility rate at 5.00% and the marginal standing facility rate at 5.50%

axis-direct-research.description: Asian markets traded mixed as investors remained cautious amid rising tensions in the Middle East and higher crude oil prices. The Hang Seng is trading higher by 0.28% while Shanghai and Nikkei are trading negative by 0.28% and 0.12%, respectively.

ssga-insights.description: A potential wave of mega-cap IPOs in 2026, including large venture-backed firms, could reshape global and US equity benchmarks.

ssga-insights.description: Structural support for gold remains intact, with prices potentially reaching $5,500/oz by year-end. Record government debt, elevated stock-bond correlations, strong physical demand, and low fund ownership underpin the outlook, though energy-driven inflation and hawkish Fed and G10 policy pose near-t

ssga-insights.title: Investing in AI through sectors
ssga-insights.description: Learn how AI is showing up across different sectors, the investment opportunities, and the implications for investors as they construct portfolios to target AI growth.

ssga-insights.title: A smarter core: An Enhanced approach to optimizing equity portfolios
ssga-insights.description: Is there a more thoughtful way to anchor your portfolio and deliver ballast—while improving the efficiency of returns beyond the traditional satellite approach? Explore an Enhanced approach to optimizing equity portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: India government bonds get a glow-up
ssga-insights.description: India has removed withholding and capital gains taxes on government bonds for foreign investors, improving access, potential returns and support for the rupee.

ssga-insights.title: Why investors should reconsider APAC weight
ssga-insights.description: APAC equities remain underweight in portfolios despite rising economic significance, offering diversification benefits and exposure to global growth drivers.

ssga-insights.title: Implementation alpha across the fixed income spectrum
ssga-insights.description: Disciplined implementation can reduce costs drag and capture inefficiencies across the fixed income spectrum from indexing to enhanced and active strategies.

ssga-insights.title: US labor strength delays Fed rate cuts
ssga-insights.description: Strong US labor data delays Fed cuts, Canada job strength supports BoC hold, ECB set to hike despite weak growth and mixed global economic signals.

ssga-insights.title: The value of dividend growth strategies to portfolios today and beyond
ssga-insights.description: In times of uncertainty, pivoting away from concentration and leveraging the stability of a dividend growth strategy with SDY can potentially help investors reduce the impact of volatility.

ssga-insights.title: Why Asia may be the biggest winner of the global AI boom
ssga-insights.description: Asia anchors the global AI ecosystem through semiconductors and cost-efficient deployment, with divergent labor outcomes and proactive tax policies shaping growth.

ssga-insights.description: Is the rise in major sovereign bond yields a temporary response to geopolitical tensions, or are markets settling into a new regime of higher rates?

ssga-insights.title: Emerging market debt: Why it belongs in your investment portfolio
ssga-insights.description: No longer a niche exposure, emerging market debt has become a key allocation that offers income, diversification, and a growing role in investment portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: ETF inflows set records as leadership narrows
ssga-insights.description: Record ETF flows highlight concentrated equity positioning and strong fixed income demand as investors navigate resilient earnings and rising macro uncertainty.

ssga-insights.description: Rising energy prices have pushed markets to reassess the policy path, lifting yields and improving front-end carry as bill issuance keeps relative value in focus.

ssga-insights.title: Emerging market debt: Enduring strength outlook holds
ssga-insights.description: Emerging market debt enters the second half of 2026 on a solid footing, with local currency bonds favored for yield, resilience, and valuation support.

ssga-insights.title: What portfolio analysis of 2025 reveals about investor behavior
ssga-insights.description: Read 7 key insights on how investors allocated assets against 2025’s volatile yet ultimately resilient market backdrop to help guide your allocations in 2026.

ssga-insights.description: Rising real yields, driven by resilient growth and Fed repricing, are reshaping markets, shifting leadership and tightening risk premia.

ssga-insights.title: US consumers strong but uneven
ssga-insights.description: Aggregate finances remain strong amid record wealth, but lower‑income consumers face rising stress that could worsen if the labor market weakens.

ssga-insights.description: How are investors using ETFs to navigate uncertainty, enhance liquidity, and diversify portfolios? Find out. Get the results of the 2025 US research study from State Street Investment Management.

ssga-insights.title: The courage to follow the fundamentals
ssga-insights.description: Look past the noise—focus on what drives markets. Growth and earnings remain strong, even as the backdrop supporting them shifts.

ssga-insights.title: Diversify for a new regime with multi-asset strategies and real assets
ssga-insights.description: As stock-bond correlations rise, diversification must evolve. Discover how multi-asset strategies and real assets can improve portfolio resilience.

ssga-insights.title: Prioritize income and resilience with active short duration and multi-sector credit
ssga-insights.description: Amid rate volatility and credit spreads remaining tight, focusing on selectivity and active positioning can help generate returns through income and carry.

ssga-insights.title: Position beyond US large-cap tech for the next wave of AI and economic realignment
ssga-insights.description: Counter market concentration by broadening your equity exposure to include US small caps, emerging markets, defense companies, and businesses at the forefront of the AI CapEx infrastructure buildout.

ssga-insights.title: 2026 Midyear ETF Market Outlook: Resilient fundamentals, rising fragility
ssga-insights.description: Earnings—the lifeblood of this bull market—continue to drive the multiyear rally in risk assets, even as the macro backdrop grows more challenging.

ssga-insights.title: Be ready for any market with liquid ETFs
ssga-insights.description: In volatile markets, liquidity is vital. And with more uncertainty likely ahead, you can get in and out quickly, easily, and at an attractive cost with State Street ETFs.

ssga-insights.title: Eurozone signals caution on June rate hike
ssga-insights.description: PMI weakness and softer inflation reshape rate paths across Europe and Japan, as demand destruction and policy shifts drive caution among major central banks.

ssga-insights.description: AI-driven gains reshape markets, but rising concentration risks challenge diversification across equities and traditional assets.

icici-bank-research.title: US CPI: Inflation increased on the back of food and energy prices
icici-bank-research.description: US CPI inflation rose above expectations in April printing at 3.8% YoY (exp: 3.7%) driven by food and energy prices. On a sequential basis, prices rose by 0.6% MoM. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: India has raised import tariffs on gold and silver to 15%, reversing the 2024 duty cuts, as the government moves to curb surging precious metal imports. Read the report to know more.

ing-think-rss.title: Airbnb only part of Belgium’s housing affordability problem
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: Short‑term rentals via platforms such as Airbnb reduce available housing supply in Belgium, adding pressure to an already tight housing market – but this is only part of the story

ing-think-rss.title: Lagarde keeps the door open for further ECB rate hikes
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde's comments at the press conference kept the door to further rate hikes open

ing-think-rss.title: Czech National Bank preview: Stubborn core inflation implies a rate hike
ing-think-rss.description: CZECH REPUBLIC: The Czech National Bank will likely proceed with a rate hike next Thursday in a split vote. We don’t consider the Czech economy to be ready for tighter monetary policy, yet hawkish rhetoric – true to Chekhov's principle – makes a hike more likely than unchanged rates. Credibility and

ing-think-rss.title: Turkish central bank holds, keeping an eye on inflationary risks
ing-think-rss.description: TURKEY: Turkey's central bank held the policy rate unchanged at 37%, which implies that it finds the current stance sufficient. While the decision signals a clear intention to preserve flexibility, the bank has remained cautious, being attentive to inflationary risks

ing-think-rss.title: ECB hikes interest rates by 25bp
ing-think-rss.description: The European Central Bank just announced the first rate hike since September 2023 in an attempt to preemptively tackle increasing inflation and to demonstrate its inflation-fighting spirit

ing-think-rss.title: ING Monthly: Already playing into extra time
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: Traditionally, the World Cup may have offered a welcome reprieve from global uncertainty and heavy headlines. ing-think-rss.title: In charts: our latest scenarios for energy prices, central banks and markets
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: Here's what happens to oil and natural gas prices if there's a quick deal or a severe re-escalation in the Middle East war – and how it would play out across financial markets

ing-think-rss.title: Energy markets remain complacent despite significant supply shock
ing-think-rss.description: With little tangible evidence of an imminent deal between the US and Iran to get energy supplies flowing through the Strait of Hormuz once again, we believe oil and gas markets are being too complacent, and see significant upside in the absence of a quick resolution

ing-think-rss.title: Our latest views on the major central banks

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why UK Gilt Yields Are Climbing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] UK government bond yields have soared amid growing political uncertainty, with questions over the future direction of fiscal policy.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: US Data Center Power Demand Projected to Double by 2027
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] US data center power demand is forecast to surge from 31 GW in 2025 to 66 GW by 2027, driven by AI expansion, with implications for regional electricity markets and grid reliability.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: AI Agents Forecast to Boost Tech Cash Flow as Usage Soars
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] Demand for Agentic AI is poised to soar at the same time costs for hyperscalers are falling, setting the stage for improved gross margins and a fresh look at the sector’s sustainability.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Korea’s Stock Market Is Forecast to Set Fresh Highs
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Goldman Sachs Research lifted its KOSPI target to 9,000, citing a 300% earnings surge and a supercycle in memory chips that could power Asia’s rally.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Securing and Financing the Future of Water
China’s Exports Are Expected to Slow Before Green Energy Surge
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] China's export growth is under near-term pressure as the Middle East energy shock dampens demand. But the crisis may accelerate global clean energy adoption, a business China dominates.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Stock Markets Are Increasingly Vulnerable to Rising Bond Yields
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] A surge in global bond yields is increasing the risk of a stock market correction, particularly if the economic backdrop deteriorates.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The S&P 500 Is Forecast to Climb as Earnings Growth Powers Stocks Higher
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Goldman Sachs Research raised its S&P 500 year-end target to 8000, projecting earnings per share of $340 in 2026 (a 24% increase, year over year). AI infrastructure investment is expected to account for about half of the earnings growth.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: How Hedge Funds Are Trading Semiconductor Stocks
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Some hedge funds are taking profits on makers of semiconductors and related equipment to manage risk in their portfolios, even as tech stocks climb.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Investors May Be Overlooking the UK’s Economic Potential
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Europe] The UK economy is beset by weak growth, soft productivity gains, persistent inflation, and pressure on fiscal policy. However the UK retains important strengths and has opportunities in the medium-term to boost economic growth, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Outlook for India’s Stock Market Amid the Middle East Conflict
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Indian equities have historically rebounded after periods of crisis, which may offer attractive entry points for long-term investors in the markets.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why Spain’s Economy Is Growing Three Times Faster Than the Euro Area
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] Spain’s strong fiscal position and ‘structural resilience’ have bolstered GDP at a time when its neighbors are struggling to cope with the global energy shock.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why the Fed Is Unlikely to Cut Rates This Year
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] Goldman Sachs Research has pushed back its forecast for Fed rate cuts to June and December 2027 (from December 2026 and March 2027 previously).

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Outlook for Data Centers in Asia
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] Traditional cloud workloads as well as new AI-first demand are driving growth in data centers in Asia, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

jpmorgan-research.title: US–Israel military operation against Iran: Are markets on edge?
jpmorgan-research.description: The conflict could pose an upside risk for oil, gold and aluminum prices — though uncertainties around its longer-term impact remain.
